title: ControlLoopTask
description: Periodic task that executes the closed-loop control algorithm. Reads latest validated ADC feedback, compares to target position, computes control output using configured gains, and sends command to actuator driver.
inputs: Validated ADC value, target position, control mode, control gains
outputs: Actuator drive command (e.g., PWM duty/command value)

title: PIDControlAlgorithm
description: Implements closed-loop control (e.g., PID) computing control output from error between target and feedback, with configurable gains and saturation handling.
inputs: Target position, feedback position, Kp/Ki/Kd, previous error, integral state

title: ADCSampler
description: Configures and triggers ADC sampling at a configurable rate between 100 Hz and 1 kHz, default 500 Hz, and publishes raw readings.

title: UARTCommandHandler
description: Parses UART command frames to set target position and control mode, validates inputs, and updates control state.
outputs: Updated target position, control mode, command acknowledgements

title: CommandParser
description: Parses incoming UART bytes into structured command messages with checksum and field validation.
outputs: Command objects (set target, set mode) or parse errors

title: UARTDiagnosticsService
description: Responds to diagnostic request commands by gathering current ADC value, target position, control output, and fault status and sending a formatted UART response.
inputs: Diagnostic request command, current system state

title: UARTDriverConfig
description: Initializes and enforces UART settings at 115200 baud, 8 data bits, no parity, 1 stop bit.

title: ConfigService
description: Provides runtime-configurable parameters for ADC sampling rate, control gains, and UART timeouts. Supports defaults and updates via commands or stored settings.

title: ConfigStore
description: Non-volatile and/or RAM storage for configuration parameters including sampling rate, gains, and timeouts with default values.

title: ADCValidationService
description: Validates ADC samples for transient faults and out-of-range values. Rejects invalid samples, increments fault counters, and triggers safe state on persistent errors.
inputs: Raw ADC samples, calibrated min/max limits
outputs: Validated ADC samples or fault indication

title: UARTFaultHandler
description: Validates UART command frames for integrity (length, checksum, value ranges) and manages fault state for invalid or malformed data.
outputs: Fault status updates, rejected command responses

title: FaultManager
description: Centralized fault state handling. Aggregates ADC and UART fault conditions and commands entry to safe state when required.

title: SafeStateController
description: Transitions actuator to a defined safe state (e.g., disable output or hold) when out-of-range ADC readings are detected.
inputs: Safe state command, current control output

title: CalibrationStore
description: Stores calibrated potentiometer min/max limits used to validate ADC range.

title: MISRA Compliance Process
description: Defines project-wide MISRA-C:2012 compliance workflow including static analysis configuration, rule enforcement, and deviation recording. Integrates tooling outputs into build artifacts and maintains a deviation register for approved exceptions.
inputs: Source code files, MISRA rule set, approved deviation list
outputs: Static analysis reports, deviation documentation artifacts

title: Supply Voltage Assumption
description: Holds constant for 12V actuator supply and provides scaling factor used by control output computation for duty cycle/drive limits.
outputs: Scaling factor for control output duty/drive limit calculations

title: ADC Channel Configuration
description: Configures a single ADC channel for actuator potentiometer feedback with required sampling time, resolution, and input pin mapping.
inputs: ADC channel ID, pin configuration, sampling settings

title: ADC Read Service
description: Reads the configured ADC channel for actuator feedback and provides raw ADC counts. Invoked by control loop each cycle.

title: Position Scaling Algorithm
description: Scales raw ADC counts to normalized position in [0.0, 1.0] using configurable min/max calibration values and clamps values outside range.

title: Control Output Calculator
description: Computes actuator control output each cycle based on measured position and commanded target. Applies scaling based on 12V assumption and output limits.
inputs: Measured position, target position, control parameters

title: Control Loop Scheduler
description: Executes the control loop at a fixed period of 10 ms with allowable jitter of ±1 ms, triggering ADC read, scaling, control calculation, and output update.

title: Feedback Fault Monitor
description: Monitors ADC feedback validity over time and disables actuator drive while reporting a fault if invalid data persists beyond 50 ms.
inputs: Raw ADC value, validity status, time since last valid reading

title: Fault Reporter
description: Registers and reports system faults to diagnostics interface and logs fault conditions for feedback invalidity.

title: Actuator Drive Output
description: Applies the computed control output to the actuator driver hardware and supports disable command from safety module.

title: ThresholdConfigRepository
description: Persists and retrieves the shipping position ADC threshold from non-volatile memory. Provides read/write access with validation of range and checksum to ensure stored value integrity.
inputs: Threshold value to persist; read request
outputs: Stored threshold value; status codes for read/write success or failure

title: ThresholdConfigService
description: Loads the shipping position threshold during initialization and serves it to control logic at runtime. Falls back to default if NVM data is invalid.

title: ShippingPositionStopLogic
description: Compares each ADC sample to the configured shipping position threshold and commands actuator stop when crossing occurs. Logic executes within the ADC sampling ISR or high-priority task to meet 20 ms stop requirement.
inputs: ADC sample stream; configured threshold; current actuator state
outputs: Stop command to actuator driver; event flag for crossing detection

title: UartCommandParser
description: Parses incoming UART frames, validates command format, and emits ON/OFF command events. Ensures receipt-to-dispatch latency within 50 ms.
outputs: Command event (ON/OFF) with timestamp; validation result

title: UartCommandParser_OFF
description: Handles OFF command parsing and dispatch in the same parsing service to trigger OFF transitions within 50 ms.

title: ActuatorStateMachine
description: Manages actuator ON/OFF states and enforces transitions triggered by UART commands. Publishes state changes to other services for acknowledgements.
outputs: Actuator drive enable/disable signals; state change events

title: ActuatorStateMachine_OFF
description: Implements OFF transition path with de-energize command to actuator driver within timing constraints.
outputs: Actuator drive disable signal; state change event

title: UartAckService
description: Sends UART acknowledgement frames for valid ON/OFF commands within 20 ms of command processing. Subscribes to state change or command validation events.
inputs: Validated command event or state change event

title: AdcDriverConfig
description: Configures the internal ADC hardware to 12-bit resolution or higher and applies sampling rate settings. Provides initialization API called at startup.

title: AdcSamplingScheduler
description: Schedules continuous ADC sampling at >=1000 samples per second using timer interrupt or DMA. Ensures no gaps longer than 2 ms by using a periodic trigger and buffering.
inputs: Timer ticks; ADC conversion complete interrupts
outputs: ADC sample stream into ring buffer

title: AdcContinuousAcquisition
description: Continuously acquires ADC samples during normal operation and feeds them to control logic. Monitors timing to ensure no gaps >2 ms and raises diagnostic flags if violated.
inputs: ADC conversion complete events; system state
outputs: Sample notifications to control logic; diagnostic status

title: ADC Sample Buffer
description: Maintains the latest ADC sample and timestamp; provides atomic read access to the most recent sample for control computations.

title: Flap Control Computation
description: Computes flap actuation commands using only the latest ADC value and configured thresholds; no other sensor inputs are consulted.

title: Actuation Range Guard
description: Enforces actuation enablement only when ADC value is within 500–2600; disables or stops actuation when outside this range.

title: Real-Time ADC Freshness Check
description: Validates that control decisions use a sample not older than 2 ms by comparing current time with latest sample timestamp before control execution.

title: Open-Loop Inhibit Gate
description: Blocks actuator drive whenever valid ADC feedback is unavailable; allows drive only if ADC is valid and fresh.

title: ADC Validity Monitor
description: Flags ADC feedback invalid if no new sample arrives within 2 ms or if value is outside 0–4095; updates validity state.

title: Drive Permission Check
description: Permits actuator drive commands only when measured ADC value is within 500–2600 inclusive; otherwise denies drive.

title: Target ADC Validator
description: Rejects configured target ADC values outside 500–2600 inclusive before storing or applying them to control logic.

title: ActuatorSafetyMonitor
description: Evaluates ADC bounds each control loop cycle and disables actuator drive when the measured ADC value is <500 or >2600. Executes within the loop timing budget to ensure action within one cycle.

title: ActuatorTargetStopController
description: Compares measured ADC to configured target and stops actuator movement when within ±1 count. Issues stop command and inhibits further drive until outside tolerance or new command.

title: SupplyVoltageADCChannelConfig
description: Initializes and configures the dedicated ADC channel connected to the external supply voltage divider with proper sampling time and reference settings.

title: SupplyVoltageScaler
description: Computes external supply voltage by applying the configured divider ratio to the raw ADC value and ADC reference scaling.

title: SupplyVoltageSampler
description: Samples the supply ADC channel at least once per control loop cycle and updates the computed supply voltage cache.

title: OvervoltageThresholdStore
description: Stores and retrieves Overvoltage_Threshold from non-volatile memory and loads it during startup for use by monitoring logic.

title: OvervoltageDetector
description: Compares computed supply voltage against Overvoltage_Threshold and raises an overvoltage condition when voltage >= threshold.

title: OvervoltageDriveDisabler
description: Disables actuator drive within one control loop cycle of overvoltage detection by acting on the overvoltage flag in the loop.

title: ActuatorCommandGate
description: Filters actuator movement commands and rejects them when the system is in overvoltage or FAULT state. The gate checks the OvervoltageMonitor status before allowing commands to reach the ActuatorController.
inputs: Actuator command object; overvoltage status flag; system state
outputs: Accepted command forwarded to ActuatorController or rejected status with reason

title: FaultTransitionHandler
description: Listens for overvoltage detection events and triggers an immediate transition to FAULT state within the next control loop cycle.
inputs: Overvoltage event; current system state; control loop tick

title: OvervoltageUartNotifier
description: Sends UART diagnostic message "OVERVOLTAGE_FAULT_ACTIVE" within 100 ms of detecting voltage >= Overvoltage_Threshold. Uses a debounce to avoid repeated transmissions until condition clears.

title: FaultStateRetentionLogic
description: Enforces FAULT state retention until voltage is within limits and a reset or authorized UART clear command is received. Validates both conditions before allowing exit.
inputs: Current voltage status; reset signal; UART clear fault command; current system state
outputs: State transition request to exit FAULT or retain FAULT

title: FaultExitVoltageChecker
description: Periodically evaluates supply voltage against acceptable limits at least every 100 ms while in FAULT state and updates eligibility to exit FAULT.
inputs: Periodic timer tick; voltage sample; acceptable voltage limits

title: OvervoltageMonitor
description: Samples supply voltage and evaluates overvoltage status at least every 100 ms across all active states. Publishes status changes to subscribers.
inputs: Voltage samples; Overvoltage_Threshold; acceptable limits; periodic timer tick
outputs: Overvoltage status flag; voltage-within-limits flag; event notifications

title: UartActuatorCommandInterface
description: Receives and parses actuator control commands over UART when link is active and forwards valid commands to the command gate.
inputs: UART byte stream; link active status
outputs: Parsed actuator command objects; error responses

title: ActuatorOnCommandParser
description: Recognizes the UART command token "ACTUATOR_ON" and maps it to an actuator enable command object with required parameters.
outputs: ActuatorCommand object of type ACTUATOR_ON or invalid command result

title: UART Command Parser
description: Parses raw UART input into command tokens and raw string payloads. Recognizes supported command strings including ACTUATOR_OFF and ACTUATOR_ON and forwards parsed results to the command dispatcher.

title: Command Dispatcher
description: Routes parsed commands to appropriate handlers. If command is ACTUATOR_ON, triggers actuator control logic within timing constraints; if ACTUATOR_OFF, triggers actuator disable; otherwise routes to invalid command handler without changing actuator state.

title: Actuator Controller
description: Implements actuator enable and disable operations. Executes configured control logic for ACTUATOR_ON and disables GPIO/PWM outputs for ACTUATOR_OFF within specified time limits.

title: Invalid Command Handler
description: Handles invalid UART commands by ensuring no actuator state change occurs and by invoking the logging service with timestamp and raw command string.

title: Invalid Command Logger
description: Logs invalid UART commands with timestamp and raw command string to persistent or ring-buffer storage.

title: Voltage Configuration Store
description: Stores nominal and operating voltage configuration values used by diagnostics and range checks. Initializes nominal voltage to 12 V.

title: Voltage Range Configuration
description: Configures acceptable supply voltage limits for operating range checks. Sets min=9 V and max=16 V for validation use.

title: Startup Safety Manager
description: Ensures all actuator control outputs (PA5 GPIO and PWM outputs) are disabled or high-impedance until initialization completes and a 100 ms post-reset stabilization delay has elapsed. Enables outputs only after delay and initialization success.

title: StartupDelayManager
description: Implements a post-reset delay gate. On system reset, it starts a hardware timer and blocks control loop scheduling and actuator enable until the elapsed time is at least 50 ms, then signals readiness.

title: ADCResolutionConfigurator
description: Configures the ADC peripheral registers to 12-bit resolution during initialization, verifying the setting and reporting status.

title: ADCReferenceConfigurator
description: Sets ADC reference voltage to 3.3 V by configuring the reference source selection and validates it against supported reference options.

title: ADCSamplingScheduler
description: Schedules ADC sampling at a minimum of 1 kHz using a hardware timer trigger or periodic task. Ensures sample period <= 1 ms and dispatches samples to processing pipeline.
outputs: ADC sample ready events, sample timestamps

title: ADCSaturationDetector
description: Tracks consecutive ADC samples equal to maximum count. If 5 consecutive maximum readings are observed, flags saturation and requests transition to FAULT state.
inputs: ADC sample value, ADC max count

title: ADCPinInitializer
description: Initializes GPIO pin PA1 in analog mode, configures it for ADC input channel mapping, and disables digital input buffers to reduce noise.

title: ActuatorGPIOInitializer
description: Configures pin PA5 as a push-pull GPIO output for actuator control and sets an initial safe output level.
inputs: Init call, pin identifier PA5, initial level

title: PWMTimerInitializer
description: Initializes an advanced PWM timer with configured frequency, duty cycle resolution, and output channel mapping to generate actuator control signals.
inputs: Init call, PWM frequency, duty cycle resolution, channel mapping

title: UART Peripheral Initializer
description: Configures UART peripheral registers (baud rate, data bits, parity, stop bits, GPIO muxing) and enables the UART clock and interrupts as required for external communication.
inputs: UART configuration structure (baud_rate, data_bits, parity, stop_bits, flow_control), UART instance identifier

title: ADC DMA Configuration Service
description: Sets up DMA controller channels for ADC data transfers, configures transfer size, circular/normal mode, memory buffer address, and enables DMA requests from ADC to reduce CPU load.
inputs: ADC handle, DMA channel config (buffer_address, length, mode, priority), sampling trigger source
outputs: DMA configuration status, DMA handle linked to ADC

title: Independent Watchdog Service
description: Enables independent watchdog with configured timeout and provides periodic refresh within 50% of timeout during normal operation; exposes refresh API used by application scheduler.
inputs: Watchdog timeout configuration, periodic refresh tick

title: Clock and Timer Configuration
description: Configures system clock source, PLL settings, and timer prescalers to produce a 1 ms control loop period within ±1% tolerance; provides timer interrupt for control loop scheduling.
inputs: Clock source config, PLL multipliers/dividers, timer target period (1 ms)
outputs: Configured system clock and timer handles, timing accuracy status

title: Layered Architecture Enforcement Interface
description: Defines abstract hardware access interfaces used by the Application layer; all hardware interactions are routed through Middleware/Driver/HAL implementations to prevent direct hardware access.
inputs: Hardware access requests (e.g., read_adc, write_uart, set_gpio)
outputs: Hardware operation results via interface methods

title: System State Machine
description: Implements finite state machine with states INIT, IDLE, ACTIVE, SHIPPING_LOCK, and FAULT; processes events/commands and triggers state transitions and entry/exit actions.
inputs: Events (reset, init_complete, activate_cmd, shipping_lock_cmd, unlock_cmd, fault_detected)

title: Reset Initialization Handler
description: On reset or power-up, forces state machine to INIT state and initiates initialization sequence.
outputs: State machine set to INIT, initialization start trigger

title: Nominal Transition Controller
description: Validates and executes nominal transitions: INIT->IDLE after initialization success, IDLE->ACTIVE on activate command, ACTIVE->SHIPPING_LOCK on shipping lock command, SHIPPING_LOCK->IDLE on unlock command.
inputs: Initialization status, command inputs (activate, shipping_lock, unlock)

title: FaultStateManager
description: Implements system state machine logic to transition to FAULT from any current state when a fault condition is flagged, and to remain in FAULT until an explicit reset event occurs. The manager checks fault flags each cycle and gates all non-reset transitions while in FAULT.
inputs: Current system state, fault_detected flag, reset_event flag
outputs: Next system state, state transition events

title: HardwareWatchdogInitializer
description: Initializes the independent hardware watchdog during system startup and configures it with the platform-supported timeout value retrieved from platform configuration or HAL.
inputs: Platform timeout value, startup lifecycle event

title: WatchdogServicer
description: Services (kicks) the independent watchdog on a periodic schedule during normal operation, ensuring at least one service within each watchdog timeout period. The service is disabled when a fatal malfunction is declared.
inputs: System run state, scheduler ticks, fatal_malfunction flag

title: FatalMalfunctionResetHandler
description: On detection of fatal software malfunction, inhibits WatchdogServicer and allows watchdog timeout to occur, triggering a system reset. Logs the malfunction cause before inhibiting servicing.

title: ADCOnlyControlAlgorithm
description: Computes closed-loop control outputs using only raw ADC feedback values and internal control state. The algorithm explicitly excludes any non-ADC sensor inputs by design, enforcing input validation at the interface boundary.
inputs: Raw ADC samples, internal controller state, configuration gains
outputs: Actuator command values, updated controller state

title: ControlLoopScheduler
description: Schedules and triggers execution of the control loop at a fixed period of 1.0 ms or less. Uses a high-resolution timer or RTOS task period to ensure ≥1 kHz execution rate.
inputs: System clock/timer ticks, configured loop period

title: ThresholdStopEvaluator
description: Evaluates raw ADC values against configured stop thresholds each control cycle and commands an actuator stop within the same cycle when thresholds are met or exceeded.

title: HysteresisBandManager
description: Applies a configurable hysteresis band around stop thresholds to prevent rapid toggling of actuator commands. Maintains last stop state and uses upper/lower bounds for re-enable decisions.
inputs: Raw ADC value, hysteresis band configuration, last stop state

title: RawAdcDataPolicy
description: Enforces use of raw ADC counts in control and decision logic by exposing only raw ADC values to control algorithms and preventing any conversion to physical units within the control path.
outputs: Raw ADC counts forwarded to control/decision logic

title: UartStatusScheduler
description: Schedules and triggers periodic UART status message transmission at a configurable interval during normal operation.
inputs: Configured interval (ms), system tick timer, normal operation flag

title: UartStatusMessageBuilder
description: Builds the UART status payload containing raw ADC value, system state, shipping lock status, and fault flags, and hands it to the UART driver for transmission.
inputs: Raw ADC counts, system state enum, shipping lock status, fault flags bitmask

title: UartConfigService
description: Configures UART peripheral with required baud rate and frame format during initialization.
inputs: UART peripheral handle, desired configuration parameters

title: UartBaudRateConfig
description: Sets UART baud rate to 115200 by computing and applying peripheral divisor values.
inputs: System clock frequency, target baud rate (115200)
outputs: UART register settings for baud rate

title: UartFrameFormatConfig
description: Configures UART frame format to 8 data bits, no parity, and 1 stop bit (8N1).
outputs: UART register settings for frame format

title: UartTransmitIntervalValidator
description: Validates configured transmit interval to be less than or equal to 100 ms and enforces fallback to 100 ms if out of range.

title: OvercurrentMonitor
description: Continuously monitors motor current and triggers actuator shutdown and fault when current exceeds configurable threshold.
inputs: Motor current ADC counts, overcurrent threshold, system tick
outputs: Actuator disable command, overcurrent fault flag

title: ThermalMonitor
description: Monitors MCU internal temperature sensor and triggers actuator shutdown and fault when temperature exceeds configurable threshold.
inputs: Temperature sensor ADC counts, thermal threshold, system tick
outputs: Actuator disable command, thermal fault flag

title: StallDetectionService
description: Detects stall by tracking commanded motion and ensuring ADC feedback changes by at least a configurable delta within a configurable duration; raises stall fault when not met.
inputs: Commanded motion state, raw ADC counts history, delta threshold, duration threshold, system tick

title: ShortCircuitDetector
description: Evaluates short-circuit indicators using either dedicated hardware flag inputs or current measurement thresholds. On detection, it requests actuator shutdown and raises a short-circuit fault in the fault manager.

title: UndervoltageMonitor
description: Monitors supply voltage from ADC samples and triggers undervoltage fault when value falls below 9 V. Ensures actuator output is disabled upon fault.

title: WatchdogService
description: Initializes and enables the hardware watchdog, periodically services it during normal execution, and on reset records a watchdog fault flag in non-volatile fault log on next boot.

title: FaultStateTransitionHandler
description: Receives fault events within the current control cycle and forces the state machine to transition to FAULT state before cycle completion. Ensures deterministic same-cycle transition.

title: TemperatureProcessing
description: Processes raw temperature sensor ADC values into engineering units using fixed-point scaling that supports -40°C to +85°C without overflow or resolution loss. Uses calibrated coefficients and saturates out-of-range values only after conversion.

title: NvmConfigStorage
description: Manages configuration and calibration data in non-volatile memory without software-enforced invalidation due to storage temperature range. Provides integrity checks only on data format/version, not temperature.

title: HumidityIndependencePolicy
description: Defines operational policy indicating no humidity sensor dependency or condensation-detection logic. Ensures build-time and runtime checks prevent use of humidity inputs.

title: AdcAcquisitionService
description: Continuously samples ADC channels at a fixed periodic rate using a timer/interrupt-driven loop. Stores the latest sample per channel in a lock-free buffer accessible by the control loop each cycle.

title: ControlLoopScheduler
description: Executes the periodic control loop. On each iteration it reads the latest ADC feedback, invokes the actuator command computation, and issues updated commands to the actuator driver.
inputs: Timer tick, latest ADC feedback sample

title: ADCReader
description: Provides the most recent ADC feedback sample from the ADC hardware abstraction and caches it for control loop use.

title: CommandCalculator
description: Computes actuator command values based on the most recent ADC feedback and control law parameters. Produces updated command signals on each loop iteration.
inputs: ADC value, control parameters, current actuator state

title: ShippingLimitGuard
description: Checks ADC feedback against the configured Shipping_ADC_Value threshold and prevents actuator motion toward the limit by issuing a stop command and inhibiting further commands in the limit direction.
inputs: ADC value, Shipping_ADC_Value threshold, requested actuator direction

title: UARTCommandHandler
description: Parses incoming UART frames and dispatches actuator ON/OFF commands. Updates actuator enable state based on valid commands.
outputs: Actuator enable state updates, command acknowledgments

title: ActuatorEnableController
description: Maintains actuator enable/disable state and applies state to the actuator driver. Ensures ON/OFF commands are reflected in hardware control signals.

title: FaultMonitor
description: Polls or receives fault input changes and detects fault conditions within 100 ms. Emits fault events upon detection.
outputs: Fault detected events with fault code

title: FaultInputProvider
description: Abstracts access to hardware or software fault inputs for the fault monitor to evaluate.
inputs: Hardware fault pins, software fault flags

title: FaultLogger
description: Logs detected faults to non-volatile storage with timestamp and fault code within 1 second of detection. Subscribes to fault events.

title: FaultLogStore
description: Non-volatile storage schema for fault log records including timestamp and fault code. Provides append-only write access.

title: SystemStateMachine
description: Controls system state transitions. Transitions from NORMAL to FAULT within 500 ms after any fault event. Maintains current state and transition causes.
inputs: Fault events, recovery commands, timer tick

title: FaultToStateTransitionService
description: Listens for fault events and triggers the SystemStateMachine to transition to FAULT within required timing constraints, providing fault cause details.

title: RecoveryCommandHandler
description: Accepts recovery commands and validates that all faults are cleared before requesting transition from FAULT to NORMAL.

title: FaultStatusTracker
description: Maintains the set of active faults and provides a query to determine whether all faults are cleared.
inputs: Fault detected events, fault cleared events

title: StateStatusProvider
description: Exposes software-accessible status indicator for current system state and last transition cause. Updated on each state transition.
inputs: State change notifications, transition cause data
outputs: State status structure for software access

title: StateStatusAPI
description: Provides read access to current system state and last transition cause for other software components.

title: ArchitectureDiagramExporter
description: Generates an exportable architecture diagram PDF by pulling architecture metadata or diagram sources from the documentation repository and rendering to PDF using a diagram renderer.

title: DocumentationRepositoryAdapter
description: Provides access to project documentation repository contents, including diagram sources and PDFs.

title: SchematicPdfExporter
description: Exports the electrical schematic to PDF using EDA tool automation or schematic source files and writes the result to the release artifacts directory.

title: PcbGerberExporter
description: Automates PCB layout export to Gerber format and stores the generated files in the release artifacts directory.

title: FirmwareReleaseTagger
description: Ensures firmware source code exists in a version-controlled repository, creates an annotated tag for the release, and captures a snapshot reference for delivery.

title: CalibrationDocIncluder
description: Validates and includes the calibration procedure PDF in the release package, copying from documentation repository or artifact store.

title: TestReportGenerator
description: Aggregates test results for a build, formats test case summaries with pass/fail status, and renders a PDF test report.

title: DemonstrationVideoIncluder
description: Validates and includes the demonstration MP4 video file in the release package, ensuring format and checksum.

title: ReleasePackageBuilder
description: Assembles exported artifacts and documentation into a structured release package directory or archive.
